heading: Definitions and special rules
label: 4662 Definitions and special rules
notes: References in TextSections 3005, 3004, and 3008 of the Solid Waste Disposal Act, referred to in subsec. (b)(8)(C)(i)(I), and section 1004 of that Act, referred to in subsec. (b)(8)(E), are classified to sections 6925, 6924, 6928, and 6903, respectively, of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare.Section 106 of the Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation, and Liability Act of 1980, referred to in subsec. (b)(8)(C)(i)(II), is classified to .section 9606 of Title 42

L. 96–510“(2).—Waiver of limitationIf refund or credit of any overpayment of tax resulting from the application of the amendments made by this section is prevented at any time before the date which for one year after the date of the enactment of this Act [] by the operation of any law or rule of law (including res judicata), refund or credit of such overpayment (to the extent attributable to the application of such amendments) may, nevertheless, be made or allowed if claim therefor is filed on or before the date which for one year after the date of the enactment of this Act.”July 18, 1984

children.label: A
children.text: which is listed in the table under section 4661(b), and

children.label: B
children.text: which is manufactured or produced in the United States or entered into the United States for consumption, use, or ware­housing.

children.heading: United States
children.label: 2 United States
children.text: The term “United States” has the meaning given such term by section 4612(a)(4).

children.heading: Importer
children.label: 3 Importer
children.text: The term “importer” means the person entering the taxable chemical for consumption, use, or warehousing.

children.heading: Ton
children.label: 4 Ton
children.text: The term “ton” means 2,000 pounds. In the case of any taxable chemical which is a gas, the term “ton” means the amount of such gas in cubic feet which is the equivalent of 2,000 pounds on a molecular weight basis.

children.heading: Fractional part of ton
children.label: 5 Fractional part of ton
children.text: In the case of a fraction of a ton, the tax imposed by section 4661 shall be the same fraction of the amount of such tax imposed on a whole ton.

children.heading: Methane or butane used as a fuel
children.label: 1 Methane or butane used as a fuel
children.text: Under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, methane or butane shall be treated as a taxable chemical only if it is used otherwise than as a fuel or in the manufacture or production of any motor fuel, diesel fuel, aviation fuel, or jet fuel (and, for purposes of section 4661(a), the person so using it shall be treated as the manufacturer thereof).

children.heading: Substances used in the production of fertilizer
children.label: 2 Substances used in the production of fertilizer

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of nitric acid, sulfuric acid, ammonia, or methane used to produce ammonia which is a qualified fertilizer substance, no tax shall be imposed under section 4661(a).

children.label: i
children.text: used in a qualified fertilizer use by the manufacturer, producer, or importer,

children.label: ii
children.text: sold for use by any purchaser in a qualified fertilizer use, or

children.label: iii
children.text: sold for resale by any purchaser for use, or resale for ultimate use, in a qualified fertilizer use.

children.heading: Qualified fertilizer use
children.label: C Qualified fertilizer use
children.text: The term “qualified fertilizer use” means any use in the manufacture or production of fertilizer or for direct application as a fertilizer.

children.heading: Taxation of nonqualified sale or use
children.label: D Taxation of nonqualified sale or use
children.text: For purposes of section 4661(a), if no tax was imposed by such section on the sale or use of any chemical by reason of subparagraph (A), the first person who sells or uses such chemical other than in a sale or use described in subparagraph (A) shall be treated as the manufacturer of such chemical.

children.heading: Sulfuric acid produced as a byproduct of air pollution control
children.label: 3 Sulfuric acid produced as a byproduct of air pollution control
children.text: In the case of sulfuric acid produced solely as a byproduct of and on the same site as air pollution control equipment, no tax shall be imposed under section 4661.

children.heading: Substances derived from coal
children.label: 4 Substances derived from coal
children.text: For purposes of this subchapter, the term “taxable chemical” shall not include any substance to the extent derived from coal.

children.heading: Substances used in the production of motor fuel, etc.
children.label: 5 Substances used in the production of motor fuel, etc.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of any chemical described in subparagraph (D) which is a qualified fuel substance, no tax shall be imposed under section 4661(a).

children.label: i
children.text: used in a qualified fuel use by the manufacturer, producer, or importer,

children.label: ii
children.text: sold for use by any purchaser in a qualified fuel use, or

children.label: iii
children.text: sold for resale by any purchaser for use, or resale for ultimate use, in a qualified fuel use.

children.label: i
children.text: any use in the manufacture or production of any motor fuel, diesel fuel, aviation fuel, or jet fuel, or

children.label: ii
children.text: any use as such a fuel.

children.heading: Chemicals to which paragraph applies
children.label: D Chemicals to which paragraph applies
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the chemicals described in this subparagraph are acetylene, benzene, butylene, butadiene, ethylene, naphthalene, propylene, toluene, and xylene.

children.heading: Taxation of nonqualified sale or use
children.label: E Taxation of nonqualified sale or use
children.text: For purposes of section 4661(a), if no tax was imposed by such section on the sale or use of any chemical by reason of subparagraph (A), the first person who sells or uses such chemical other than in a sale or use described in subparagraph (A) shall be treated as the manufacturer of such chemical.

children.heading: Substance having transitory presence during refining process, etc.
children.label: 6 Substance having transitory presence during refining process, etc.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: No tax shall be imposed under section 4661(a) on any taxable chemical described in subparagraph (B) by reason of the transitory presence of such chemical during any process of smelting, refining, or otherwise extracting any substance not subject to tax under section 4661(a).

children.heading: Chemicals to which subparagraph (A) applies
children.label: B Chemicals to which subparagraph (A) applies
i
children.text: barium sulfide, cupric sulfate, cupric oxide, cuprous oxide, lead oxide, zinc chloride, and zinc sulfate, and

children.label: ii
children.text: any solution or mixture containing any chemical described in clause (i).

children.heading: Removal treated as use
children.label: C Removal treated as use
children.text: Nothing in subparagraph (A) shall be construed to apply to any chemical which is removed from or ceases to be part of any smelting, refining, or other extraction process.

children.heading: Special rule for xylene
children.label: 7 Special rule for xylene
children.text: Except in the case of any substance imported into the United States or exported from the United States, the term “xylene” does not include any separated isomer of xylene.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: No tax shall be imposed under section 4661(a) on any chromium, cobalt, or nickel which is diverted or recovered in the United States from any solid waste as part of a recycling process (and not as part of the original manufacturing or production process).

children.heading: Exemption not to apply while corrective action uncompleted
children.label: B Exemption not to apply while corrective action uncompleted
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply during any period that required corrective action by the taxpayer at the unit at which the recycling occurs is uncompleted.

children.label: I
children.text: a final permit under section 3005 of the Solid Waste Disposal Act or a final order under section 3004 or 3008 of such Act, or

children.label: II
children.text: a final order under section 106 of the Comprehensive Environmental Response, Compensation, and Liability Act of 1980, and

children.label: ii
children.text: ending on the date the Administrator or such State (as the case may be) certifies to the Secretary that such corrective action has been completed.

children.heading: Special rule for groundwater treatment
children.label: D Special rule for groundwater treatment
children.text: In the case of corrective action requiring groundwater treatment, such action shall be treated as completed as of the close of the 10-year period beginning on the date such action is required if such treatment complies with the permit or order applicable under subparagraph (C)(i) throughout such period. The preceding sentence shall cease to apply beginning on the date such treatment ceases to comply with such permit or order.

children.heading: Solid waste
children.label: E Solid waste
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “solid waste” has the meaning given such term by section 1004 of the Solid Waste Disposal Act, except that such term shall not include any byproduct, coproduct, or other waste from any process of smelting, refining, or otherwise extracting any metal.

children.heading: Substances used in the production of animal feed
children.label: 9 Substances used in the production of animal feed
i
children.text: nitric acid,

children.label: ii
children.text: sulfuric acid,

children.label: iii
children.text: ammonia, or

children.label: iv
children.text: methane used to produce ammonia,

children.label: i
children.text: used in a qualified animal feed use by the manufacturer, producer, or importer,

children.label: ii
children.text: sold for use by any purchaser in a qualified animal feed use, or

children.label: iii
children.text: sold for resale by any purchaser for use, or resale for ultimate use, in a qualified animal feed use.

children.heading: Qualified animal feed use
children.label: C Qualified animal feed use
children.text: The term “qualified animal feed use” means any use in the manufacture or production of animal feed or animal feed supplements, or of ingredients used in animal feed or animal feed supplements.

children.heading: Taxation of nonqualified sale or use
children.label: D Taxation of nonqualified sale or use
children.text: For purposes of section 4661(a), if no tax was imposed by such section on the sale or use of any chemical by reason of subparagraph (A), the 1st person who sells or uses such chemical other than in a sale or use described in subparagraph (A) shall be treated as the manufacturer of such chemical.

children.heading: Hydrocarbon streams containing mixtures of organic taxable chemicals
children.label: 10 Hydrocarbon streams containing mixtures of organic taxable chemicals

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: No tax shall be imposed under section 4661(a) on any organic taxable chemical while such chemical is part of an intermediate hydrocarbon stream containing one or more organic taxable chemicals.

children.heading: Removal, etc., treated as use
children.label: B Removal, etc., treated as use
i
children.text: such isolation, extraction, removal, or cessation shall be treated as use by the person causing such event, and

children.label: ii
children.text: such person shall be treated as the manufacturer of such chemical.

children.heading: Registration requirement
children.label: C Registration requirement
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to any sale of any intermediate hydrocarbon stream unless the registration requirements of clauses (i) and (ii) of subsection (c)(2)(B) are satisfied.

children.heading: Organic taxable chemical
children.label: D Organic taxable chemical
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “organic taxable chemical” means any taxable chemical which is an organic substance.

children.heading: Use and certain exchanges by manufacturer, etc.
children.label: c Use and certain exchanges by manufacturer, etc.

children.heading: Use treated as sale
children.label: 1 Use treated as sale
children.text: Except as provided in subsections (b) and (e), if any person manufactures, produces, or imports any taxable chemical and uses such chemical, then such person shall be liable for tax under section 4661 in the same manner as if such chemical were sold by such person.

children.label: i
children.text: such exchange shall not be treated as a sale, and

children.label: ii
children.text: such other person shall, for purposes of section 4661, be treated as the manufacturer, producer, or importer of such chemical.

children.label: i
children.text: both parties are registered with the Secretary as manufacturers, producers, or importers of taxable chemicals, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the person receiving the taxable chemical has, at such time as the Secretary may prescribe, notified the manufacturer, producer, or importer of such person’s registration number and the internal revenue district in which such person is registered.

children.heading: Inventory exchange
children.label: C Inventory exchange
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “inventory exchange” means any exchange in which 2 persons exchange property which is, in the hands of each person, property described in section 1221(a)(1).

children.heading: Refund or credit for certain uses
children.label: d Refund or credit for certain uses
A
children.text: a tax under section 4661 was paid with respect to any taxable chemical, and

children.label: B
children.text: such chemical was used by any person in the manufacture or production of any other substance which is a taxable chemical,

children.label: A
children.text: a tax under section 4661 was paid with respect to nitric acid, sulfuric acid, ammonia, or methane used to make ammonia without regard to subsection (b)(2), and

children.label: B
children.text: any person uses such substance as a qualified fertilizer substance,

children.label: A
children.text: a tax under section 4661 was paid with respect to any chemical described in subparagraph (D) of subsection (b)(5) without regard to subsection (b)(5), and

children.label: B
children.text: any person uses such chemical as a qualified fuel substance,

children.heading: Use in the production of animal feed
children.label: 4 Use in the production of animal feed
A
children.text: a tax under section 4661 was paid with respect to nitric acid, sulfuric acid, ammonia, or methane used to produce ammonia, without regard to subsection (b)(9), and

children.label: B
children.text: any person uses such substance as a qualified animal feed substance,

children.heading: Exemption for exports of taxable chemicals
children.label: e Exemption for exports of taxable chemicals

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: No tax shall be imposed under section 4661 on the sale by the manufacturer or producer of any taxable chemical for export, or for resale by the purchaser to a second purchaser for export.

children.heading: Proof of export required
children.label: B Proof of export required
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of section 4221(b) shall apply for purposes of subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Credit or refund where tax paid
children.label: 2 Credit or refund where tax paid
i
children.text: tax under section 4661 was paid with respect to any taxable chemical, and

children.label: I
children.text: such chemical was exported by any person, or

children.label: II
children.text: such chemical was used as a material in the manufacture or production of a substance which was exported by any person and which, at the time of export, was a taxable substance (as defined in section 4672(a)),

children.label: i
children.text: has repaid or agreed to repay the amount of the tax to the person who exported the taxable chemical or taxable substance (as so defined), or

children.label: ii
children.text: has obtained the written consent of such exporter to the allowance of the credit or the making of the refund.

children.label: A
children.text: the person who paid the tax waives his claim to the amount of such credit or refund, and

children.label: B
children.text: the person exporting the taxable chemical or taxable substance provides such information as the Secretary may require in such regulations.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 4 Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary to carry out the purposes of this subsection.

children.heading: Disposition of revenues from Puerto Rico and the Virgin Islands
children.label: f Disposition of revenues from Puerto Rico and the Virgin Islands
children.text: The provisions of subsections (a)(3) and (b)(3) of section 7652 shall not apply to any tax imposed by section 4661.
